attacking_style: Commits to extremely long-range solo or small group breakaways (190km documented), willing to spend entire race off the front. Thrives in reduced sprint scenarios where attrition favors survivalists over pure sprinters.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support; relies on individual initiative rather than coordinated setups. Functions as opportunistic breakaway specialist rather than protected leader.
terrain_preference: Excels in attritional Classics conditions (cobbles, reduced bunch scenarios). Exposed in headwind situations where solo efforts prove futile despite exceptional endurance capacity.
vulnerability: Solo breakaways in adverse wind conditions render stamina irrelevant; lacks firepower to hold off organized chases. Reputation deficit may limit counterattack responses from peloton.
key_patterns: Executes ultra-distance breakaways exceeding 190km, showing exceptional stamina for extended efforts.
Achieves best results when attrition creates reduced finales, not pure bunch sprints.
Functions as unknown/underestimated rider, allowing breakaway survival when favorites mark each other.
Demonstrates persistence over calculated tactics; willingness to suffer outweighs strategic positioning.
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Paris-Roubaix fourth place represents significant breakthrough result, suggesting improved ability to convert long efforts into results in chaotic finales
